title: Empirical model/effort slash-inject + JSONL-persistence probes (live claude sessions)

tests.summary: Two committed, re-runnable live-session probes. slash-inject: spawned Sonnet 4.6@high, injected '/model haiku' -> status line moved to 'Haiku 4.5' (switch confirmed). jsonl: ran one turn (scraped reply 'PONG'), session project dir created but contains NO <sid>.jsonl (only a memory/ subdir) and no transcript written anywhere in the window.

typecheck.summary: 0 errors (probes are standalone .mjs; no existing-code churn)

e2e.summary: N/A — the probes ARE the live verification; committed scripts are the re-runnable artifacts

notes: slash-inject-verdict: WORKS — injecting `/model haiku` via writeKeys into a live operative session moved the status-line model Sonnet 4.6@high -> Haiku 4.5. The status line also exposes effort (e.g. 'Sonnet 4.6@high'). DESIGN CONSEQUENCE: Stage B (MR1d) and the warm pool (MR0d) use ONE generic pool with `/model` + `/effort` injection at checkout (~1s), NOT per-{model,effort} respawn. (haiku showed 'auto mode unavailable for this model' — effort tiers apply only where the model supports them, matching the brief.) jsonl-verdict: ABSENT — a claude-pty-spawned operative turn scraped its reply from the screen ('PONG') but claude 2.1.175 wrote NO conversation transcript: the project dir ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/ was created (holds only memory/) but no <sessionId>.jsonl. This confirms screen.mjs's empirical claim and resolves the exploration's open contradiction for THIS spawn shape. DESIGN CONSEQUENCE: route telemetry stays script-call-primary (the gateway writes decisions.jsonl at resolution time; the reply `[route:]` token is read from the screen scrape, never transcript-parsed). Satisfies brief §3 tokens slash-inject-verdict + jsonl-verdict.
